title: Remove old login item code
author: nikwen
body:
Electron 44 will remove support for macOS 12.

Login items use a new macOS API on macOS 13+ and an old one on macOS 12 and below.

Because we no longer support macOS 12, we can remove the code that uses the old API.

This is where it is implemented:

https://github.com/electron/electron/blob/97af1d3854f2a22716542827ad44b04b5d30e02b/shell/browser/browser_mac.mm#L458-L479

title: [Electron Documentation] `app-region` change to `window-drag`
author: kyerebo
body:
### Preflight Checklist

- [x] I have read the [Contributing Guidelines](https://github.com/electron/electron/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) for this project.
- [x] I agree to follow the [Code of Conduct](https://github.com/electron/electron/blob/main/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md) that this project adheres to.
- [x] I have searched the [issue tracker](https://www.github.com/electron/electron/issues) for a bug report that matches the one I want to file, without success.

### Electron Version

43.0.0

### What operating system(s) are you using?

Windows

### Operating System Version

N/A

### What arch are you using?

Other (specify below)

### Last Known Working Electron version

_No response_

### Does the issue also appear in Chromium / Google Chrome?

No

### Expected Behavior

Chromium recently standardized window-drag as the successor to app-region as part of new CSS WG resolutions to standardized spec text: https://drafts.csswg.org/css-ui-4/#window-drag. While `app-region` is still supported on Chromium in the interim for interoperability, it may be fully deprecated in the future once `window-drag` takes root. Does Electron plan to support the new property name, and if it already does, should the docs be updated accordingly?

### Actual Behavior

Currently, the docs for [Custom Window Interactions](https://www.electronjs.org/docs/latest/tutorial/custom-window-interactions) show `app-region` instead of `window-drag`.

### Testcase Gist URL

_No response_

### Additional Information

_No response_

title: ready-to-show never fires on Windows 11 Insider Preview build 26200 (25H2) with NVIDIA GPU
author: Kide-Lee
body:
### Preflight Checklist

- [x] I have read the [Contributing Guidelines](https://github.com/electron/electron/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) for this project.
- [x] I agree to follow the [Code of Conduct](https://github.com/electron/electron/blob/main/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md) that this project adheres to.
- [x] I have searched the [issue tracker](https://www.github.com/electron/electron/issues) for a bug report that matches the one I want to file, without success.

### Electron Version

33.4.11

### What operating system(s) are you using?

Windows

### Operating System Version

Windows 11 Insider Preview, build 26200 (25H2)

### What arch are you using?

x64

### Last Known Working Electron version

_No response_

### Does the issue also appear in Chromium / Google Chrome?

I don't know how to test

### Expected Behavior

> **Bug 排查与 Issue 撰写**：eva-cli (Electronic Video Agent)
> **排查日期**：2026-07-14
> **提交目标**：https://github.com/electron/electron/issues

`ready-to-show` fires after the page is ready, and the window becomes visible.


### Actual Behavior

`ready-to-show` **never fires**. The window is created but remains invisible forever.

`did-finish-load` fires normally, confirming the renderer process is alive and the page loaded successfully.


### Testcase Gist URL

_No response_

### Additional Information

## Root Cause Analysis

A custom Electron diagnostic tool was built to isolate the root cause by running 5 automated tests, each creating different types of `BrowserWindow` (basic, GPU-accelerated + WebGL, offscreen, WebView, `app.getGPUInfo()`).

### Diagnostic Results: 5/5 Passed (with workaround)

All 5 tests passed, but **only because each test used `did-finish-load` instead of `ready-to-show`** to determine success. This confirms the rendering pipeline is functional — the problem is specifically that the `ready-to-show` signal is never emitted.

### Two Underlying Failures Identified

**1. GPU Shader Disk Cache Initialization Failure**

Every Chromium process start triggers:

```
cache_util_win.cc: GetDiskCacheDir() → Access Denied
```

The GPU shader disk cache directory cannot be created under `%APPDATA%`, causing the GPU process to fail during initialization.

**2. Steam CEF Confirms Chromium-Level Bug**

Steam's CEF (`steamwebhelper.exe`) is also affected — CEF browser and utility processes crash-loop indefinitely (~10 second cycle). Verified via `debug.log`:

```
[0714/013847.821:INFO:crash_reporting.cc(238)] Crash reporting enabled for process: browser
[0714/013847.888:INFO:crash_reporting.cc(238)] Crash reporting enabled for process: utility
[0714/013857.970:INFO:crash_reporting.cc(238)] Crash reporting enabled for process: browser
[0714/013858.038:INFO:crash_reporting.cc(238)] Crash reporting enabled for process: utility
... (repeats indefinitely, every ~10 seconds)
```

This proves the issue is at the **Chromium level**, not Electron-specific.

## Workarounds (Verified)

| Severity | Flags | Effect |
|----------|-------|--------|
| Light | `--disable-gpu-shader-disk-cache` | Bypasses cache init failure; GPU acceleration preserved |
| Medium | `--disable-gpu --disable-gpu-sandbox --in-process-gpu` | Disables GPU entirely; confirmed working for Obsidian & Unity Hub |
| Heavy | `--no-sandbox --disable-gpu` | Last resort; removes all sandboxing |

### Applying Workarounds

For end-user Electron apps (no source access), add flags to the shortcut target:

```
"D:\soft\Obsidian\Obsidian.exe" --disable-gpu --disable-gpu-sandbox --in-process-gpu
```

For app developers, use `app.commandLine.appendSwitch()` before `app.whenReady()`:

```js
app.commandLine.appendSwitch('disable-gpu');
app.commandLine.appendSwitch('disable-gpu-sandbox');
app.commandLine.appendSwitch('in-process-gpu');
```

## Affected Applications (Verified)

| Application | Type | Status |
|-------------|------|--------|
| Obsidian | Electron | Fixed with workaround |
| Unity Hub | Electron | Fixed with workaround |
| Steam | CEF | Not fixable locally (Valve code signing prevents parameter injection) |

## Notes

- This appears to be a **Windows 11 Insider Preview build 26200 specific regression** — the same apps work correctly on stable Windows 11 builds (23H2/24H2)
- The issue is **not specific to NVIDIA** in theory, but only NVIDIA GPU systems have been tested
- `wmic` (WMI command-line) is also broken on this build, suggesting broader system-level issues
- No related Chromium bug tracker entries or GitHub issues were found during investigation

title: Renderer CHECK crash in blink::V8MetricsRecorder::GetUkmRecorderAndSourceId during full GC when a service-worker preload realm context exists (MV3 extension SW)
author: kazkondo
body:
### Preflight Checklist

- I have read the [Contributing Guidelines](https://github.com/electron/electron/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) for this project.
- I agree to follow the [Code of Conduct](https://github.com/electron/electron/blob/main/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md) that this project adheres to.
- I have searched the issue tracker for a bug report that matches the one I want to file, without success.

### Electron Version

42.5.2

### What operating system(s) are you using?

macOS

### Operating System Version

macOS 26.0.1 (25A362), arm64

### What arch are you using?

arm64 (including Apple Silicon)

### Last Known Working Electron version

_Unknown — likely none since service-worker preload realms were introduced_

### Expected Behavior

A renderer process hosting a chrome-extension MV3 background service worker, with a service-worker preload script registered via `session.registerPreloadScript({ type: 'service-worker', ... })`, survives V8 full GC cycles.

### Actual Behavior

The renderer crashes with `EXC_BREAKPOINT` (CHECK failure) whenever a **full GC cycle** completes in the service-worker isolate while the Electron **service-worker preload realm context** is among the isolate's contexts. In our production browser (bundled MV3 extension whose SW does frequent `ipcRenderer.invoke` work) this reproduces reliably within minutes-to-hours of browsing; we have a series of Crashpad minidumps, all identical.

Symbolicated crashing stack (Electron 42.5.2 dSYM, Chromium 148.0.7778.271), innermost first:

```
0  <ICF-deduplicated function>                                  (crash, EXC_BREAKPOINT)
1  blink::V8MetricsRecorder::GetUkmRecorderAndSourceId          v8_metrics.cc:662
2  blink::V8MetricsRecorder::AddMainThreadEvent(v8::metrics::GarbageCollectionFullCycle const&, ContextId)
3  v8::internal::GCTracer::StopCycle
4  v8::internal::GCTracer::NotifyFullSweepingCompletedAndStopCycleIfFinished
5  v8::internal::Heap::EnsureSweepingCompleted
6  v8::internal::Heap::CollectGarbage
7  v8::internal::HeapAllocator::CollectGarbageAndRetryAllocation
8  v8::internal::HeapAllocator::AllocateRawSlowPath
9  v8::internal::Factory::NewJSObject / NewJSPromiseWithoutHook
10 v8::Promise::Resolver::New
11 gin_helper::PromiseBase::PromiseBase                          promise.cc:66
12 (anonymous namespace)::IPCBase<IPCServiceWorker>::Invoke      (electron_api_ipc SW binding)
```

The renderer command line includes `--service-worker-preload`.

### Analysis

`V8MetricsRecorder::GetUkmRecorderAndSourceId` resolves the GC event's `ContextId` back to a `v8::Context` and then calls `blink::ExecutionContext::From(context)` → `context->UkmRecorder()` (v8_metrics.cc:660-663 in Chromium 148):

- For blink-created contexts this is fine.
- For **Electron's service-worker preload realm context**, the blink embedder-data slot (`kV8ContextPerContextDataIndex`) does not contain a blink `ScriptState`. `ExecutionContext::From` therefore returns a garbage pointer (the release-build `DCHECK`s are compiled out), and the subsequent virtual call `context->UkmRecorder()` traps (CHECK/CFI → `EXC_BREAKPOINT`).

So any full-GC metrics event whose context id maps to the preload realm context kills the renderer. Nothing the embedding app does in JS can prevent it; heavy SW workloads (allocation pressure → full GC) make it frequent.

Downstream consequence worth noting: the dead SW gets restarted by extension event delivery, so an app can easily end up in a crash-restart loop (we saw sustained 100% CPU in `ServiceWorkerVersion::StartWorker → AllocateWorkerProcess` in the browser process until force-quit).

### Suggested fix direction

Exclude non-blink (preload realm) contexts from blink's per-context V8 metrics, e.g. ensure the preload realm context's embedder data makes `ExecutionContext::From` return `nullptr` (the function already null-checks), or set `v8::Context` embedder fields such that `v8::metrics::Recorder::GetContext` cannot resolve to the realm context.

### Testcase Gist URL

_No minimal gist yet — the crash needs an MV3 extension SW + service-worker preload script + enough allocation to trigger a full GC. Happy to provide the full Crashpad minidumps and hang reports privately, or to work on a minimal repro if that helps._

### Additional Information

- Also affects universal (x64/arm64) builds; observed on arm64.
- Electron 42.6.x release notes show no related fix; the relevant Chromium code (`third_party/blink/renderer/bindings/core/v8/v8_metrics.cc`) is unchanged in 148.0.7778.271.

title: WebFrameMain.postMessage throws synchronously on disposed frame — asymmetric with send()/executeJavaScript, no 'destroyed' event lets consumers unsubscribe
author: seaman333
body:
### Preflight Checklist

- [x] I have read the [Contributing Guidelines](https://github.com/electron/electron/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) for this project.
- [x] I agree to follow the [Code of Conduct](https://github.com/electron/electron/blob/main/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md) that this project adheres to.
- [x] I have searched the [issue tracker](https://www.github.com/electron/electron/issues) for a bug report that matches the one I want to file, without success.

### Electron Version

30+

### What operating system(s) are you using?

Windows

### Operating System Version

Windows 10 22H2, build 19045.6466

### What arch are you using?

x64

### Last Known Working Electron version

Not applicable — bug is architectural (contract asymmetry). PR #31401 is a partial mitigation for the sticky-disposed sub-case only

### Does the issue also appear in Chromium / Google Chrome?

I don't know how to test

### Expected Behavior

`WebFrameMain.postMessage`, sync property accessors, and `executeJavaScript` on a disposed frame should degrade **uniformly** — either all silently drop (parity with `send()`), or all reject a promise, or all fire a JS-visible event that lets defensive consumers unsubscribe before the next microtask can dispatch. Whichever is chosen, the same disposed frame should not produce three different failure modes across three call sites in the same JS session.

Additionally: a `WebFrameMain` should emit a `'frame-disposed'` (or `'destroyed'`) event when `MarkRenderFrameDisposed()` runs, giving RxJS pipelines a `takeUntil(fromEvent(frame, 'frame-disposed'))` primitive that closes the race at the app-code layer without TOCTOU-vulnerable polling.

### Actual Behavior

`postMessage` and every sync accessor throw the exact string `"Render frame was disposed before WebFrameMain could be accessed"` via `gin_helper::ErrorThrower::ThrowError()`. The JS wrapper for `postMessage` in `lib/browser/api/web-frame-main.ts` has no try/catch (`send()` does, `postMessage` doesn't). The throw escapes the caller's callback, and in the RxJS-pipeline case tears down the subscription that fed the UI, producing a permanently blank Docker Desktop dashboard while the engine backend runs normally.

### Testcase Gist URL

_No response_

### Additional Information

## Summary

`WebFrameMain.prototype.postMessage` throws a synchronous V8 exception (`"Render frame was disposed before WebFrameMain could be accessed"`) when a caller-held wrapper is dispatched to after the underlying `RenderFrameHost` has been disposed. The throw is undefendable from downstream code because:

1. The disposal contract is **asymmetric** across the WebFrameMain API surface:
   - `send()` swallows the throw into `console.error` (JS-wrapper try/catch)
   - `postMessage()` propagates
   - `executeJavaScript()` rejects a promise
   - Sync accessors (`.url`, `.origin`, `.processId`, `.frameToken`, `.name`, `.routingId`, `.top`, `.parent`, `.frames`, `.framesInSubtree`, `.visibilityState`, `.osProcessId`) throw synchronously
2. No JS-visible `'destroyed'` / `'disposed'` event fires on the WebFrameMain instance — `MarkRenderFrameDisposed()` only flips a bool + tears down the Mojo pipe. Downstream consumers have no first-class signal to unsubscribe on; `isDestroyed()` is TOCTOU on the UI thread that also runs `MarkRenderFrameDisposed`.
3. Consequence: any consumer that captures a `WebFrameMain` reference and dispatches to it from a delayed callback (RxJS `debounceTime`/`auditTime`/`delay`/`observeOn(asapScheduler)`, `mergeMap`/`switchMap` over async work, `setTimeout`, promise continuation) is guaranteed to hit `CheckRenderFrame()` on a stale wrapper whenever the RFH turned over in between. The throw propagates out of the callback and tears down its host subscription/observer — for RxJS, the subscription transitions to `_error` and the source is unsubscribed forever, wedging any UI dependent on that pipeline in an empty state.

## Environment

I don't run Electron directly. The bug reaches me via Docker Desktop for Windows (all versions 4.66 – 4.75.0, still open in 4.81.0), whose UI is a downstream Electron 30+ app. Verified against Docker Desktop's shipped `app.asar` — `frontend/build/entry-cli.main.js` contains `notificationUpdates` (RxJS observable name) × 4, `onNotificationChange` (minified subscriber) × 10, `postMessage` × 4. Docker's `package.json` lists `rxjs` as a dependency (`workspace:catalog`).

- Electron version: whichever Docker Desktop 4.75 bundles (>= 30 based on PR #31401 being present)
- Windows 10 22H2 build 19045.6466
- x64
- WSL2 backend, Docker Desktop UI (Electron main process)

## Expected Behavior

`WebFrameMain.postMessage`, sync property accessors, and `executeJavaScript` on a disposed frame should degrade **uniformly** — either all silently drop (parity with `send()`), or all reject a promise, or all fire a JS-visible event that lets defensive consumers unsubscribe before the next microtask can dispatch. Whichever is chosen, the same disposed frame should not produce three different failure modes across three call sites in the same JS session.

Additionally: a `WebFrameMain` should emit a `'frame-disposed'` (or `'destroyed'`) event when `MarkRenderFrameDisposed()` runs, giving RxJS pipelines a `takeUntil(fromEvent(frame, 'frame-disposed'))` primitive that closes the race at the app-code layer without TOCTOU-vulnerable polling.

## Actual Behavior

`postMessage` and every sync accessor throw the exact string `"Render frame was disposed before WebFrameMain could be accessed"` via `gin_helper::ErrorThrower::ThrowError()`. The JS wrapper for `postMessage` in `lib/browser/api/web-frame-main.ts` has no try/catch (`send()` does, `postMessage` doesn't). The throw escapes the caller's callback, and in the RxJS-pipeline case tears down the subscription that fed the UI, producing a permanently blank Docker Desktop dashboard while the engine backend runs normally.

## Root cause — file + line hints (against `main` branch)

Throw site — `shell/browser/api/electron_api_web_frame_main.cc`:

```cpp
// L207-216
bool WebFrameMain::CheckRenderFrame() const {
  if (!HasRenderFrame()) {
    v8::Isolate* isolate = JavascriptEnvironment::GetIsolate();
    v8::HandleScope scope(isolate);
    gin_helper::ErrorThrower(isolate).ThrowError(
        "Render frame was disposed before WebFrameMain could be accessed");
    return false;
  }
  return true;
}

// L186-190 — flag flip triggered from WebContents::RenderFrameDeleted
void WebFrameMain::MarkRenderFrameDisposed() {
  render_frame_detached_ = true;
  render_frame_disposed_ = true;
  TeardownMojoConnection();
  // <-- no Unpin(), no Emit(), no in-flight cancellation
}
```

Pin/Unpin asymmetry — same file, L611 pins wrapper at construction; L184 unpins only in `Destroyed()`. `MarkRenderFrameDisposed()` doesn't unpin, so a caller-held JS reference keeps the disposed wrapper reachable indefinitely.

Callers into disposed state — L363-394 (`PostMessage`) and every property callback installed by `FillObjectTemplate` at L619-645 all route through `CheckRenderFrame()` and propagate the throw. `Send()` alone routes through `lib/browser/api/web-frame-main.ts` which try/catches into `console.error` — that asymmetry is the wire tripped by downstream consumers.

Sibling mitigation applied only in one place — L538-562 (`CollectedJavaScriptCallStack`) has an inline disposal re-check inside its reply callback and rejects with the sibling string `"Render frame was disposed before call stack was received"`. This is direct evidence the maintainers know the mid-call race exists and have applied a narrow mitigation — extending that discipline to the general dispatch surface is what this issue asks for.

Related — `UpdateRenderFrameHost` at L192-205 resets `render_frame_disposed_ = false` on cross-origin swap (PR #31401). This closes the sticky-disposed-across-swap sub-case, but **strictly worsens** a sibling class: a cached reference that survives an `UpdateRenderFrameHost` between two RxJS ticks silently retargets a *different* renderer process with no error at all. Any fix here should be paired with a `'frame-swapped'` event so consumers can distinguish "still my frame" from "a new frame now lives here".

## Trigger sequence (Docker Desktop's specific path — generalisable)

1. Consumer captures `webContents.mainFrame` (a WebFrameMain instance) in a closure — either directly, or via a service/BehaviorSubject that later dispatches to it.
2. Renderer restarts (engine reconnect / BFCache eviction / cross-origin nav / WebContents recreate).
3. Chromium's `content::WebContentsImpl` fires `RenderFrameDeleted` for the old RFH → Electron's `WebContents::RenderFrameDeleted` (in `shell/browser/api/electron_api_web_contents.cc` ~L2070-2087) calls `MarkRenderFrameDisposed()`.
4. Any RxJS operator with a scheduler yield (`debounceTime` / `auditTime` / `delay` / `observeOn` / async `mergeMap` / `switchMap`) between emit and dispatch opens a race window — the disposal callback runs on the same UI thread that drains microtasks, so the flag flip can interleave between yield and resume.
5. Next tick dispatches `frame.postMessage(...)` (or reads a sync accessor to route by URL/origin/processId).
6. `CheckRenderFrame()` throws → propagates → RxJS `_next` → `_error` → subscription torn down.
7. No further notifications reach the UI. Blank.

## Proposed fix

Two-part upstream fix. Part A closes the immediate throw path; Part B provides the primitive downstream consumers need for correct patterns going forward. Both are additive; neither is a public-API break for the disposal-hostile call sites (postMessage's behaviour change from throw to warn-and-drop matches send()'s existing contract).

### Part A — `PostMessage` warn-and-drop parity with `Send`

`shell/browser/api/electron_api_web_frame_main.cc` around L363:

```cpp
void WebFrameMain::PostMessage(v8::Isolate* isolate, ...) {
  if (!HasRenderFrame()) {
    // Rate-limit warnings per frame — some consumers will retry many times
    // during a single disposal window.
    static base::flat_set<int> warned_frame_ids;
    if (warned_frame_ids.insert(frame_tree_node_id_).second) {
      LOG(WARNING) << "postMessage() to disposed WebFrameMain (drop)";
    }
    return;
  }
  // ... existing implementation
}
```

Rationale: matches `Send()`'s existing swallow-into-`console.error` contract already implemented in `lib/browser/api/web-frame-main.ts`. Documents in the drop the exact call site (avoids silent-dispatch-to-nowhere debugging pain).

**Deliberately NOT changed**: sync property accessors continue to throw. Silently defaulting `.url` / `.origin` / `.processId` / `.frameToken` would allow consumers to silently misroute dispatch to `""` / `undefined`, a strictly worse failure mode than the throw.

### Part B — emit `'frame-disposed'` event on `MarkRenderFrameDisposed`

Same file, L186:

```cpp
void WebFrameMain::MarkRenderFrameDisposed() {
  render_frame_detached_ = true;
  render_frame_disposed_ = true;
  Emit("frame-disposed");   // <-- new event, additive, no ABI change
  TeardownMojoConnection();
}
```

This gives downstream consumers the primitive they currently have to synthesize from lower-granularity WebContents-level events (`did-start-navigation` / `destroyed`), none of which cleanly cover subframe deletion, BFCache eviction, or WebContents-recreate paths. RxJS pipelines can then use:

```ts
frame.postMessage(channel, payload);
// paired with:
notificationUpdates$
  .pipe(takeUntil(fromEvent(frame, 'frame-disposed')))
  .subscribe(...);
```

## Follow-up (out of scope for this issue but noted)

A companion `'frame-swapped'` event on `UpdateRenderFrameHost` (L192-205) would close the residual silent-retarget class introduced by PR #31401 — a cached reference that survives an RFH swap between two callbacks would give consumers a first-class signal to invalidate. Willing to file separately.

## Downstream impact

Docker Desktop 4.66 – 4.75+ blank-UI regression on Windows. Same class of hazard is latent in every Electron consumer that captures a WebFrameMain across an RxJS/observable/promise-chain scheduler boundary — Slack, VS Code, Discord, Signal, Notion, GitHub Desktop are all plausible carriers. A platform fix here unblocks everyone.

## References

- PR #31401 / #31425 — the related but distinct fix (sticky-disposed-across-swap only)
- `CollectedJavaScriptCallStack` L538-562 — sibling method with the mitigation this issue asks to generalise
- (Fill in from PR #31401's author, `@benhillis`, and reviewers if they're still active)

title: `nativeTheme.shouldUseDarkColorsForSystemIntegratedUI` has an incorrect (inverted) value after the 'updated' event
author: panther7
body:
### Preflight Checklist

- [x] I have read the [Contributing Guidelines](https://github.com/electron/electron/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) for this project.
- [x] I agree to follow the [Code of Conduct](https://github.com/electron/electron/blob/main/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md) that this project adheres to.
- [x] I have searched the [issue tracker](https://www.github.com/electron/electron/issues) for a bug report that matches the one I want to file, without success.

### Electron Version

41.10.1, 42.6.1, 43.1.0

### What operating system(s) are you using?

Windows

### Operating System Version

Windows 11 25H2

### What arch are you using?

x64

### Last Known Working Electron version

39.0.0-beta.3

### Does the issue also appear in Chromium / Google Chrome?

I don't know how to test

### Expected Behavior

The first call to `nativeTheme.shouldUseDarkColorsForSystemIntegratedUI` returns the correct value, as described in the [docs](https://www.electronjs.org/docs/latest/api/native-theme#nativethemeshouldusedarkcolorsforsystemintegratedui-macos-windows-readonly).

The value read inside the `nativeTheme` `'updated'` event listener is inverted.

```js
function isDark() {
  console.log('isDark', nativeTheme.shouldUseDarkColorsForSystemIntegratedUI)
}

nativeTheme.on('updated', () => isDark()) // inverted value...
isDark() // ok
```

### Actual Behavior

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/9cd24aca-3f89-4e8d-822f-0534e83ecf78

### Testcase Gist URL

https://gist.github.com/c75f4f0c02dfdbad8e410cf650b5d6b5

### Additional Information

_No response_

title: App crashes with `crashed -1073741819` and no other console output when `locales/` directory is empty
author: poran-dip
body:
### Preflight Checklist

- [x] I have read the [Contributing Guidelines](https://github.com/electron/electron/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) for this project.
- [x] I agree to follow the [Code of Conduct](https://github.com/electron/electron/blob/main/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md) that this project adheres to.
- [x] I have searched the [issue tracker](https://www.github.com/electron/electron/issues) for a bug report that matches the one I want to file, without success.

### Electron Version

43.1.0

### What operating system(s) are you using?

Windows

### Operating System Version

Windows 11 25H2

### What arch are you using?

x64

### Last Known Working Electron version

_No response_

### Does the issue also appear in Chromium / Google Chrome?

No

### Expected Behavior

When no locale data is available for the app's target locale, Electron should either log a clear diagnostic (e.g. `Error: No locale file found for "en"`) before the renderer fails, or surface a specific reason via `render-process-gone` — not fail silently with only a bare exit code.

### Actual Behavior

The app crashes on launch with only this single line printed to console:
```text
RENDERER GONE: crashed -1073741819
```
No stack trace, no `uncaughtException`, no additional detail from `render-process-gone`, and nothing else logged from the main process. After extensive testing, this is confirmed to be the *only* output ever produced under this failure mode.

### Testcase Gist URL

_No response_

### Additional Information

This isn't a bug in application code, so it isn't reproducible as an Electron Fiddle gist. It surfaces from a *packaging-time* misconfiguration: `electron-builder` accepts an invalid `electronLanguages` value (`["en"]` instead of `["en-US"]`) and silently ships a build with an empty `locales/` directory, with no error or warning at build time. That's tracked as a bundler-side bug here: [electron-userland/electron-builder#10006](https://github.com/electron-userland/electron-builder/issues/10006).

This issue is scoped specifically to Electron's *runtime* behavior once that state exists: when `locales/` is empty, the app crashes with no diagnostic information whatsoever, regardless of how it got that way (bad bundler config, manual deletion, corrupted install, etc.). Since the failure mode is state-based rather than code-based, the clearest minimal repro is a standalone project rather than a Fiddle:

https://github.com/poran-dip/electron-crash

Steps: 
1. `git clone https://github.com/poran-dip/electron-crash.git`
2. `pnpm install`
3. `pnpm build`
4. run the packaged app
5. observe the crash with no console output beyond the exit code.

Exit code `-1073741819` corresponds to `0xC0000005` (STATUS_ACCESS_VIOLATION), suggesting a native-level crash when the renderer attempts to read nonexistent locale data, rather than a graceful failure.

Environment:
- Electron: 43.1.0
- Windows 11, version 25H2 (build 10.0.26200)
- node: 24.18.0

title: [Feature Request]: per-session opt-out for the Touch ID WebAuthn platform authenticator
author: bnfy
body:
# Feature Request: per-session opt-out for the Touch ID WebAuthn platform authenticator

### Preflight Checklist

- [x] I have read the Contributing Guidelines for this project.
- [x] I agree to follow the Code of Conduct that this project adheres to.
- [x] I have searched the issue tracker for a feature request that matches the one I want to file, without success.

### Problem Description

`app.configureWebAuthn({ touchID: { keychainAccessGroup } })` enables the macOS Touch ID platform authenticator globally: `ElectronWebAuthenticationDelegate::GetTouchIdAuthenticatorConfig` reads a static access group, so every `Session` — including non-persistent ones — gets a working platform authenticator.

The credential *metadata secret*, however, is per-`BrowserContext`: it's read from (or generated into) the context's `PrefService` (`shell/browser/webauthn/electron_authenticator_request_delegate.cc`). For an in-memory session (`session.fromPartition('private')` without `persist:`), that secret is regenerated on every launch. The result for a browser-style app with private/incognito windows:

- A passkey **created** in the non-persistent session is written to the Secure Enclave / keychain permanently, but its metadata can never be unsealed after the app quits — the credential is orphaned forever, and the keychain item leaks (accumulates) with no way for the site or user to know.
- The UX is silently broken: the ceremony succeeds, the site believes the user has a passkey, and after relaunch sign-in fails with no explanation.

Apps can't defend against this today:

- The access group is app-global static state — there is no per-session configuration, so a private session can't be excluded.
- Renderer-side interception of `navigator.credentials.create()` is bypassable and wrong-layer.
- Calling `app.configureWebAuthn({})` dynamically around private-session requests would race concurrent normal-session ceremonies.

### Proposed Solution

Any of these would solve it (in rough order of preference):

1. A per-session switch, e.g. `session.setWebAuthnTouchIdEnabled(false)` or an option on `fromPartition`, honored by returning `std::nullopt` from `GetTouchIdAuthenticatorConfig` for that context.
2. `app.configureWebAuthn` accepting a session filter/predicate.
3. At minimum: skip the Touch ID authenticator for contexts whose `PrefService` is in-memory (i.e. never able to persist the metadata secret), or document the hazard in the `configureWebAuthn` docs.

### Alternatives Considered

Documented the behavior to users as "passkeys created in private tabs are lost on quit" (shipping that today), but the orphaned Secure Enclave keychain items still accumulate and the site-side UX remains misleading.

### Additional Information

Observed on Electron 43.1.0 in a browser shell (one default session + one non-persistent session for private tabs), macOS, signed build with the `keychain-access-groups` entitlement + embedded Developer ID provisioning profile. Happy to PR this if maintainers agree on the API shape.

title: [Bug]: macOS main process crashes with NSInvalidArgumentException when notification icon attachment creation fails
author: litujia1007
body:
### Preflight Checklist

- [x] I have read the [Contributing Guidelines](https://github.com/electron/electron/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) for this project.
- [x] I agree to follow the [Code of Conduct](https://github.com/electron/electron/blob/main/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md) that this project adheres to.
- [x] I have searched the [issue tracker](https://www.github.com/electron/electron/issues) for a bug report that matches the one I want to file, without success.

### Electron Version

42.5.2

### What operating system(s) are you using?

macOS

### Operating System Version

macOS 26.5.1 (25F80)

### What arch are you using?

arm64

### Last Known Working Electron version

41 (any version before the UserNotifications framework rewrite in #47817)

### Expected Behavior

If creating the `UNNotificationAttachment` for the notification icon fails (e.g. the image cannot be persisted to disk), the notification should be delivered without the icon, and the app should not crash.

### Actual Behavior

The browser process crashes with an uncaught Objective-C exception before the notification is delivered:

```
*** Terminating app due to uncaught exception 'NSInvalidArgumentException',
reason: '*** -[__NSPlaceholderArray initWithObjects:count:]: attempt to insert nil object from objects[0]'

Thread 0 Crashed (CrBrowserMain):
  +[NSArray arrayWithObjects:count:]
  Electron Framework ...
  __CFRUNLOOP_IS_CALLING_OUT_TO_A_SOURCE0_PERFORM_FUNCTION__
  ...
  NSApplicationUncaughtExceptionHandler → SIGTRAP (exit code 133)
```

**Root cause** — in [`shell/browser/notifications/mac/cocoa_notification.mm`](https://github.com/electron/electron/blob/v42.5.2/shell/browser/notifications/mac/cocoa_notification.mm), the attachment factory (image task runner) explicitly returns `nil` on failure:

```objc
return (attachment && !error) ? attachment : nil;
```

…but the reply callback on the UI thread uses the result without a nil check:

```objc
content.attachments = @[ attachment ];  // @[nil] → NSInvalidArgumentException
```

`NotificationImageRetainer::RegisterTemporaryImage` writes the icon under `<user data dir>/Notification Resources/`; any I/O failure there (`CreateDirectory` / `CreateTemporaryFileInDir` / `WriteFile`), or `+[UNNotificationAttachment attachmentWithIdentifier:URL:options:error:]` returning an error, produces the `nil` that crashes the process.

We captured two production crashes of our app with identical stacks at this location (long-running instance in the background; an incoming message triggered a notification with an icon), and then reproduced it deterministically.

This is a regression from the UserNotifications framework rewrite in #47817 (first shipped in v42); the pre-rewrite `NSUserNotification` path used `setContentImage:` and had no attachment array. The code on `main` is unchanged and has the same problem.

I'm happy to submit a PR — the fix is a nil-guard that falls back to delivering the notification without the attachment.

### Testcase Gist URL

https://gist.github.com/litujia1007/0cffd6eacaf21fb5d9b7c3f248af8936

### Additional Information

The gist is deterministic: it occupies the `<userData>/Notification Resources` path with a plain file so the image retainer's `CreateDirectory` fails, then shows a notification with an icon. On 42.5.2 (darwin/arm64) it crashes with the exception above before delivery (exit code 133). Removing the `icon` option makes the exact same scenario survive.

title: `autoUpdater` stops being able to auto update on macOS after 100 log files exist
author: nikwen
body:
As reported here by @shirakaba:

* https://github.com/Squirrel/Squirrel.Mac/issues/322
* https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/310927#issuecomment-4909789239
* https://x.com/birch_js/status/2074647024478441792
* https://x.com/birch_js/status/2074647567049334880

What’s interesting is that most apps don’t keep multiple log files. Slack, for example, only keeps one `ShipIt_stdout.log`. In the screenshot from the tweet, we see 100 different ones.

_(Sorry for not using the proper issue template. I wanted to make sure we have a tracking bug for it and I don't know their info from the template such as OS version, etc. Happy to replace if someone wants to file with the proper template.)_

title: [Bug]: chrome.webRequest is schema-registered but has no bindings in MV3 service workers, crashing worker registration
author: dep
body:
### Preflight Checklist

- [X] I have read the [Contributing Guidelines](https://github.com/electron/electron/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) for this project.
- [X] I agree to follow the [Code of Conduct](https://github.com/electron/electron/blob/main/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md) that this project adheres to.
- [X] I have searched the [issue tracker](https://www.github.com/electron/electron/issues) for a bug report that matches the one I want to file, without success.

### Electron Version

43.0.0

### What operating system are you using?

macOS

### Operating System Version

macOS 15 (Darwin 25.5.0)

### What arch are you using?

arm64 (including Apple Silicon)

### Last Known Working Electron version

_No response_

### Expected Behavior

An MV3 extension with the `webRequest` permission can register observational listeners (e.g. `chrome.webRequest.onBeforeRequest.addListener(...)`) in its background service worker, as it can in Chrome.

### Actual Behavior

The `webRequest` API feature/schema is registered — `typeof chrome.webRequest === 'object'` inside the worker — but the renderer bindings module resources are missing from the resource bundle:

```
ERROR:extensions/renderer/resource_bundle_source_map.cc:72] NOTREACHED hit. Module resource registered as "webRequest" not found
ERROR:extensions/renderer/resource_bundle_source_map.cc:72] NOTREACHED hit. Module resource registered as "webRequestEvent" not found
"(BLESSED_EXTENSION context for <id>) No source for require(webRequest)"
```

As a result `chrome.webRequest` is an empty object, `chrome.webRequest.onBeforeRequest` is `undefined`, the worker throws at top level, and MV3 service worker registration fails entirely:

```
Uncaught TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'addListener')
Service worker registration failed. Status code: 15
```

Because the registration failure takes the whole worker down, any real-world extension whose worker touches `chrome.webRequest` at startup is completely non-functional (observed with NordPass, whose action button is driven by its worker). This half-registered state is arguably worse than the API being absent — extensions that feature-detect `chrome.webRequest` pass the check and then crash.

Full log: see the gist.

### Testcase Gist URL

https://gist.github.com/dep/ec59df61562cfb3af21031a660e733b4

### Additional Information

- Follow-up to the MV3 support push in #37876 (https://github.com/electron/electron/issues/37876#issuecomment-1656808686) — service worker registration, `chrome.scripting`, and `chrome.tabs` all work great now; `chrome.webRequest` appears to be the next-most-impactful gap for MV3 extensions.
- `chrome.declarativeNetRequest` is entirely `undefined` in the same context (expected, since it is not on the supported-API list, but noting it since MV3 network-observing extensions typically need one of the two).
- Prior art: #2115 (2016 request for `chrome.webRequest.*`, closed), #19447 (//extensions integration tracking issue, closed).
- Two plausible resolutions from the outside: (a) pack the `webRequest`/`webRequestEvent` custom-bindings resources and wire the browser-side `WebRequestAPI`/event router so the API actually works, or (b) stop registering the schema so the API is cleanly absent and feature-detection in extensions behaves. (a) is obviously the dream. 🙏

title: Allow monospaced digit fonts for menu item labels on macOS
author: thomasbachem
body: ### Preflight Checklist - [x] I have read the [Contributing Guidelines](https://github.com/electron/electron/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) for this project. - [x] I agree to follow the [Code of Conduct](https://github.com/electron/electron/blob/main/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md) that this project adheres to. - [x] I have searched the [issue tracker](https://www.github.com/electron/electron/issues) for a feature request that matches the one I want to file, without success. ### Problem Description On macOS, menu item labels are rendered with the system menu font, whose digits are proportional. So any menu showing a column of numbers comes out jagged, because e.g. `11:15` renders narrower than `09:45`: There's currently no way around this: `MenuItem.label` maps to `NSMenuItem.title`, and Electron exposes neither `attributedTitle` nor any font option. In a native app this is a one-liner: set `attributedTitle` with `monospacedDigitSystemFontOfSize:`, the SF variant with tabular figures that's otherwise identical to the menu font. #22363 solved the same problem for the tray title with `tray.setTitle(title, { fontType: 'monospacedDigit' })`. Menu item labels never got the equivalent, so a tray calendar can align its countdown but not the time column in its dropdown. ### Proposed Solution The same `fontType` option on `MenuItem`, macOS-only like `toolTip`: ```js new MenuItem({ label: "11:15 Doctor's appointment", fontType: 'monospacedDigit' }) ``` The implementation looks small: when the option is set, `electron_menu_controller.mm` renders the label via `attributedTitle` with the corresponding font variant, analogous to the `fontType` handling in `tray_icon_cocoa.mm`. Supporting both tray values (`monospaced` and `monospacedDigit`) keeps the two APIs symmetric. ### Alternatives Considered - Padding with figure spaces (U+2007): Still unequal widths, the digits themselves are proportional - Unicode fullwidth/monospace digits: Wrong font, looks out of place - Pre-rendering the time column into template `NativeImage` icons: What we ship today – works well, but adds a lot of machinery for one line of AppKit ### Additional Information I attached [menu-item-font-type.patch](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/29671470/menu-item-font-type.patch) mirroring the `toolTip` per-item plumbing and the tray `fontType` implementation. It was drafted with Claude Fable 5, and I'm currently unable to build Electron locally – so it's untested. Happy to open a PR from it so CI can validate it, or for a maintainer to adopt or rework it instead.

title: [Bug]: macOS: every keystroke in any input/textarea recreates a native autofill popup NSWindow, blocking the browser process 100-260 ms per key on macOS 26 (worst with CJK IME)
author: hirorocky
body:
### Preflight Checklist

- [x] I have read the [Contributing Guidelines](https://github.com/electron/electron/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) for this project.
- [x] I agree to follow the [Code of Conduct](https://github.com/electron/electron/blob/main/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md) that this project adheres to.
- [x] I have searched the [issue tracker](https://www.github.com/electron/electron/issues) for a bug report that matches the one I want to file, without success.

### Electron Version

42.3.3 (the relevant code is unchanged on `main`)

### What operating system(s) are you using?

macOS

### Operating System Version

macOS 26.4.1 (25E253)

### What arch are you using?

arm64 (Apple Silicon)

### Last Known Working Electron version

Unknown — the popup churn exists in all recent versions; it became user-visible on macOS 26, where window ordering waits on synchronous WindowServer transactions.

### Expected Behavior

Typing into an `<input>` or `<textarea>` that has no datalist suggestions should not create or destroy native windows, and should not block the browser process.

### Actual Behavior

Every keystroke that changes the value of a focused `<input>`/`<textarea>` (with the caret at the end) makes the renderer send `ElectronAutofillDriver::ShowAutofillPopup` **with an empty suggestion list**, and the browser process destroys and recreates a native autofill popup `NSWindow` each time. On macOS 26, each of those window-ordering operations blocks `CrBrowserMain` for 50–350 ms in a synchronous WindowServer transaction. Since `CrBrowserMain` routes all input events, **every window and input of the app stutters**.

CJK IME users are hit hardest: apps that type into a hidden textarea (e.g. xterm.js terminals) never change the textarea value for plain keys (keydown is `preventDefault()`ed), but **IME preedit lives in the textarea and changes its value on every keystroke** — so composition triggers the churn on every key. This was discovered while investigating Japanese-IME typing jank in [Orca](https://github.com/stablyai/orca) (an Electron-based terminal/agent app), where the jank affects the whole UI while composing.

Root cause chain (all verified):

1. **Renderer** — `AutofillAgent::ShowSuggestions` (`shell/renderer/electron_autofill_agent.cc`) reaches `ShowPopup(element, {}, {})` for any editable with a non-empty value and caret at end, **even when there are zero datalist suggestions** — including `<textarea>`, which can never have any. `TextFieldValueChanged` fires per keystroke (and per `compositionupdate` during IME composition).
2. **Browser** — `AutofillDriver::ShowAutofillPopup` (`shell/browser/electron_autofill_driver.cc`) calls `AutofillPopup::CreateView` (`shell/browser/ui/autofill_popup.cc`), which unconditionally `Hide()`s the previous popup and creates + shows a new native child window — even for an empty list. Net effect: one NSWindow destroy + create + order-in per keystroke.
3. **macOS 26** — `sample(1)` of the browser process during typing shows `CrBrowserMain` blocked in:

   ```text
   -[NSWindow _doOrderWindow:]
     -[NSCGSWindow(NSCGSWindowOrdering) isOrderedIn]
       SLSWindowIsOrderedIn (SkyLight)
         SLSConnectionSynchronizeSLSCATransaction
           _SLSTransactionWaitSource → mach_msg2_trap   ← 1961 of 2006 samples
   ```

Evidence from a real 90 s Japanese-typing session in Orca (`devtools.timeline` + `toplevel` + `latencyInfo` trace): 74 `Receive mojo message` tasks ≥ 30 ms tagged `electron.mojom.ElectronAutofillDriver` on `CrBrowserMain`, totalling **10.1 s** with a **263 ms** max; 15 of 23 keydowns with > 100 ms `InputLatency::RawKeyDown` overlapped one of those tasks. Renderer-side handlers took 1–2 ms — the latency is entirely browser-process dispatch delay.

### Testcase Gist URL

https://gist.github.com/hirorocky/82b35c2604e29b80883921b806a711ac

### Additional Information

The testcase needs no IME: it synthesizes composition updates via `webContents.debugger` (`Input.imeSetComposition`) into a plain autofocused `<textarea>`, and measures browser-process main-thread stalls with a 16 ms timer-drift monitor (the Node event loop shares `CrBrowserMain`, so synchronous WindowServer waits appear as timer drift).

Results on macOS 26.4.1 / Electron 42.3.3 / M-series:

```text
# textarea focused (autofill popup churn):
50 synthetic IME composition updates in 6957 ms
main-process event-loop stalls >50ms: count=37 total=4915 ms max=350 ms

# CONTROL=1 (textarea blurred, no editable target → no autofill traffic):
50 synthetic IME composition updates in 5220 ms
main-process event-loop stalls >50ms: count=1 total=118 ms max=118 ms
```

Manual repro: run the same app and type into the textarea with a Japanese/Chinese/Korean IME — the Event Timing API logs keydown durations in the hundreds of ms.

The same measurement against **VS Code 1.125.1 (Electron 42.2.0)** on the same machine reproduces identically — VS Code ships the same code and is not immune: 25 synthetic composition updates into its integrated terminal (a real xterm.js helper textarea) produced 26 `ElectronAutofillDriver` tasks totalling **1081 ms (max 163 ms)** on CrBrowserMain; a plain `<input>` in its workbench produced 333 ms. VS Code's *editor* escapes only because Monaco keeps the caret mid-value in its hidden textarea, which fails the `requires_caret_at_end` check.

Causality was also confirmed in-app with the caret condition alone: keeping a zero-width sentinel after the caret during composition (so `SelectionEnd() != value.length()`) drops the same 25-keystroke workload from **1208 ms to 0 ms** of browser-main blocking — the messages still flow, but the cheap `HideAutofillPopup` path replaces the popup-window rebuild.

Why editors like VS Code (Monaco) escape this: Monaco keeps surrounding context in its hidden textarea with the caret mid-value, so the `requires_caret_at_end` check in `ShowSuggestions` fails and only the cheap `HidePopup` path runs. xterm.js keeps the caret at the end of the preedit, hitting the expensive path on every composition update.

**Proposed fix** — mirror Chromium and only show the popup when there are suggestions:

```cc
// shell/renderer/electron_autofill_agent.cc, AutofillAgent::ShowSuggestions
  std::vector<std::u16string> data_list_values;
  std::vector<std::u16string> data_list_labels;
  if (!input_element.IsNull()) {
    GetDataListSuggestions(input_element, &data_list_values, &data_list_labels);
  }
  if (data_list_values.empty()) {
    HidePopup();
    return;
  }
  ShowPopup(element, data_list_values, data_list_labels);
```

`AutofillPopup::Hide()` is already a no-op when nothing is showing, so the steady-state cost for suggestion-less fields becomes zero. A belt-and-suspenders guard in `AutofillDriver::ShowAutofillPopup` (`if (values.empty()) { autofill_popup_->Hide(); return; }`) would additionally protect against compromised renderers.

This is likely a meaningful contributor to the broader "Electron apps feel sluggish on macOS 26" class of reports (e.g. #48311 discusses SLS transaction sensitivity): the per-keystroke popup churn predates Tahoe but used to be cheap; Tahoe's synchronous WindowServer transactions turned it into user-visible input latency. Full `sample(1)` output and the 90 s trace are retained and can be shared on request.

title: Windows 11 (build 26200) + AMD RX 9070: Chromium GPU process fails during startup (`GPU process isn't usable. Goodbye.`)
author: NeytirX
body:
### Preflight Checklist

- [x] I have read the [Contributing Guidelines](https://github.com/electron/electron/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) for this project.
- [x] I agree to follow the [Code of Conduct](https://github.com/electron/electron/blob/main/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md) that this project adheres to.
- [x] I have searched the [issue tracker](https://www.github.com/electron/electron/issues) for a bug report that matches the one I want to file, without success.

### Electron Version

Electron 39.8.3

### What operating system(s) are you using?

Windows

### Operating System Version

25H2

### What arch are you using?

x64

### Last Known Working Electron version

_No response_

### Does the issue also appear in Chromium / Google Chrome?

I don't know how to test

### Expected Behavior

Electron applications should launch normally.


Maybe duplicate of : https://github.com/electron/electron/issues/52098
https://github.com/electron/electron/issues/51761

Affected applications:

* Obsidian 1.12.7
* Signal Desktop 8.17.0
* Discord (latest)

Not affected:

* Visual Studio Code 1.126.0

## Environment

* OS: Windows 11 Pro
* Build: 26200.8737
* GPU: AMD Radeon RX 9070
* Driver: 32.0.31021.5001
* Secondary GPU: AMD Radeon(TM) Graphics






### Actual Behavior

## Actual Behavior

Multiple Electron applications terminate during startup.

The logs consistently end with:

```text
[FATAL:content\browser\gpu\gpu_data_manager_impl_private.cc:415]
GPU process isn't usable. Goodbye.
```

Signal additionally reports repeated GPU process failures before aborting:

```text
GPU process exited unexpectedly: exit_code=-2147483645
```

Windows Event Viewer records Application Error (Event ID 1000) with exception code:

```
0x80000003
```

The faulting module is the application executable itself (e.g. `Obsidian.exe`, `Signal.exe`).

## Reproduction

Launching Obsidian normally:

```powershell
& "C:\Users\<user>\AppData\Local\Programs\Obsidian\Obsidian.exe"
```

Results in:

* application exits almost immediately
* log ends with `GPU process isn't usable. Goodbye.`

The same behavior is observed with Signal Desktop and Discord.

## Startup Flag Testing

### `--in-process-gpu`

Launching with:

```text
--in-process-gpu
```

changes the behavior:

* the process no longer exits immediately
* however, the application window is not rendered correctly (GUI is missing / blank)

### Four-flag combination

Launching with:

```text
--disable-gpu
--disable-gpu-compositing
--disable-software-rasterizer
--in-process-gpu
```

changes the behavior further.

In Safe Mode, this combination allows the UI to render instead of immediately terminating.

Outside Safe Mode, startup behavior changes, but rendering is still incomplete or absent.

The fact that `--in-process-gpu` consistently changes the failure mode suggests the issue is related to Chromium's GPU process initialization.

## Diagnostics Already Performed

* Updated AMD driver to latest available version
* `sfc /scannow`
* Windows Defender scan
* Malwarebytes scan
* HDR disabled
* Safe Mode testing
* AMD Radeon Software terminated
* DxDiag reports no display or DirectX problems

None of the above resolved the issue.

If there are additional Chromium or Electron logging flags that would help diagnose why the GPU process exits, I would be happy to collect and provide them.

### Testcase Gist URL

`npx electron`

### Additional Information

Visual Studio Code launches normally on the same machine.

This suggests the issue is not a complete Electron failure, but may depend on the Electron/Chromium version or GPU initialization path used by different applications.

### Actual Behavior

title: Replacing menu while open causes segfault on Windows
author: rf-figma
body:
### Preflight Checklist

- [x] I have read the [Contributing Guidelines](https://github.com/electron/electron/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) for this project.
- [x] I agree to follow the [Code of Conduct](https://github.com/electron/electron/blob/main/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md) that this project adheres to.
- [x] I have searched the [issue tracker](https://www.github.com/electron/electron/issues) for a bug report that matches the one I want to file, without success.

### Electron Version

43.0.0

### What operating system(s) are you using?

Windows

### Operating System Version

Windows 10.0.19045 (6466)

### What arch are you using?

x64

### Last Known Working Electron version

41.2.0

### Does the issue also appear in Chromium / Google Chrome?

No

### Expected Behavior

Calling `Menu.setApplicationMenu()` while the user interacts with an open menu should not crash the app.

### Actual Behavior

If the application menu is replaced while being displayed, and a GC triggers before the menu closes, the relevant `Menu` object will be garbage-collected, causing various kinds of segfaults in menu-related UI code.

For example:
```
OS Version: Windows 10.0.19045 (6466)
Report Version: 104

Crashed Thread: 38224

Application Specific Information:
Fatal Error: EXCEPTION_ACCESS_VIOLATION_READ / 0x0

Thread 38224 Crashed:
0   Figma.exe                       0x7ff6274073c7      ui::MenuModel::GetModelAndIndexForCommandId (menu_model.cc:49)
1   Figma.exe                       0x7ff62a4fd728      views::MenuModelAdapter::GetLabelFontList (menu_model_adapter.cc:225)
2   Figma.exe                       0x7ff62a507cdf      views::MenuItemView::GetFontList (menu_item_view.cc:1005)
3   Figma.exe                       0x7ff62a505f35      views::MenuItemView::OnPaintImpl (menu_item_view.cc:1115)
4   Figma.exe                       0x7ff620987cfb      views::View::Paint (view.cc:1475)
5   Figma.exe                       0x7ff620988261      views::View::RecursivePaintHelper (view.cc:3114)
6   Figma.exe                       0x7ff62098811f      views::View::PaintChildren (view.cc:2436)
7   Figma.exe                       0x7ff62a502328      views::SubmenuView::PaintChildren (submenu_view.cc:316)
8   Figma.exe                       0x7ff620987d6f      views::View::Paint (view.cc:1481)
9   Figma.exe                       0x7ff620988261      views::View::RecursivePaintHelper (view.cc:3114)
10  Figma.exe                       0x7ff62098811f      views::View::PaintChildren (view.cc:2436)
11  Figma.exe                       0x7ff620987d6f      views::View::Paint (view.cc:1481)
12  Figma.exe                       0x7ff620988261      views::View::RecursivePaintHelper (view.cc:3114)
13  Figma.exe                       0x7ff62098811f      views::View::PaintChildren (view.cc:2436)
14  Figma.exe                       0x7ff620987d6f      views::View::Paint (view.cc:1481)
15  Figma.exe                       0x7ff620988261      views::View::RecursivePaintHelper (view.cc:3114)
16  Figma.exe                       0x7ff62098811f      views::View::PaintChildren (view.cc:2436)
17  Figma.exe                       0x7ff620987d6f      views::View::Paint (view.cc:1481)
18  Figma.exe                       0x7ff620988261      views::View::RecursivePaintHelper (view.cc:3114)
19  Figma.exe                       0x7ff62098811f      views::View::PaintChildren (view.cc:2436)
20  Figma.exe                       0x7ff620987d6f      views::View::Paint (view.cc:1481)
21  Figma.exe                       0x7ff622ce48fe      views::View::PaintFromPaintRoot (view.cc:3122)
22  Figma.exe                       0x7ff622d60191      ui::Layer::PaintContentsToDisplayList (layer.cc:1599)
```

### Testcase Gist URL

https://gist.github.com/rf-figma/103e9c2d491975cbefe183bfa5ca3cc5

### Additional Information

To trigger the issue in Fiddle, you need to run the app with `--js-flags=--expose-gc`, open the File menu, and mouse over the menu a bit.

This issue was introduced in 41.2.1, almost definitely in #50915 (the backport of #50465).

That PR contains a bunch of GC-related changes, one of which was the removal of a `gin_helper::SelfKeepAlive` property from `Menu`. I think [the premise of the change was that this was redundant](https://github.com/electron/electron/pull/50806#pullrequestreview-4079112260), since `Menu` instances are all owned by other objects that should retain them properly.

The problem with this is that the owner of a `Menu` will only retain it as long as it hasn't been replaced. If you replace the menu while it's open, and the `Menu` object gets garbage-collected as a result, its destructor will clear all of the delegate pointers that are still being used by the rendered views.

I tried just restoring the `SelfKeepAlive` and it did fix the issue for me, although I'm not sure if there's some reason that we would need to do this differently now. I'll put up a PR in a second.

title: desktopCapturer returns sources with empty `display_id` on Windows
author: michalzaq12
body:
### Preflight Checklist

- [x] I have read the [Contributing Guidelines](https://github.com/electron/electron/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) for this project.
- [x] I agree to follow the [Code of Conduct](https://github.com/electron/electron/blob/main/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md) that this project adheres to.
- [x] I have searched the [issue tracker](https://www.github.com/electron/electron/issues) for a bug report that matches the one I want to file, without success.

### Electron Version

43.0.0

### What operating system(s) are you using?

Windows

### Operating System Version

Windows 11

### What arch are you using?

x64

### Last Known Working Electron version

_No response_

### Does the issue also appear in Chromium / Google Chrome?

No

### Expected Behavior

Sources returned by desktopCapturer should have a valid `display_id` property when using a capturer other than DirectX on Windows. There is a bug in code https://github.com/electron/electron/blob/57cc00ce06d2a1dc222bb2c358dcd82cfd57ab13/shell/browser/api/electron_api_desktop_capturer.cc#L485 for Windows path `display_id` is only initlialized when `using_directx_capturer_ = true `. Moreover `using_directx_capturer_` should be set based on actual usage, now it only checks if DirectX capturer is supported, while GDI Capturer can be used as a fallback. This will also cause errors in the future for the WGC capturer. 

### Actual Behavior

`display_id = ''`

### Testcase Gist URL

https://gist.github.com/michalzaq12/52be3585397f508a3c4c67f5c14f8004

### Additional Information

_No response_
